component.name: Collapsible
component.description: Toggle visibility of a content section.

useCases: Single standalone expand/collapse — 'Show more', 'View raw JSON', 'Advanced settings'
Progressive disclosure on a card or panel — reveal additional detail without navigating
Expandable sidebar sections where only one toggle controls one content block
Collapsible code blocks, detail panels, or supplementary context

commonPatterns.name: basic-collapsible
commonPatterns.description: Simple toggle to reveal or hide content

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Collapsible for multiple related sections that the user picks between
antiPatterns.reason: Collapsible is a single standalone toggle with no sibling awareness. It cannot coordinate open/close state across sections or provide mutual exclusion.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Accordion for grouped collapsible sections — it handles shared context, mutual exclusion, and keyboard navigation between items.

antiPatterns.scenario: Wrapping multiple Collapsibles in a div to simulate accordion behavior
antiPatterns.reason: You lose mutual exclusion, arrow key navigation, and shared state. Accordion is not 'multiple Collapsibles in a div' — it's a separate primitive with group behavior.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Accordion with autoCollapse for single-expand behavior, or autoCollapse={false} for independent panels.

antiPatterns.scenario: Hiding critical content behind a Collapsible by default
antiPatterns.reason: Users may miss important information if it starts collapsed
antiPatterns.alternative: Show critical content by default; use defaultOpen for important sections

slots.CollapsibleContent: The content that is shown or hidden

accessibility.role: Radix provides aria-expanded on trigger and manages content visibility
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Enter/Space to toggle; Tab to navigate
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus stays on trigger after toggling

rules: Always include both CollapsibleTrigger and CollapsibleContent
Use Accordion instead when multiple collapsible sections need coordinated behavior
Consider defaultOpen for content that should be visible on initial render

aiHints.context: Use for a single standalone expand/collapse — one trigger, one content panel, no relationship to other sections. If you have multiple related sections where the user picks between them, that's Accordion. Collapsible is a primitive; Accordion is a group. Supports controlled (open/onOpenChange) and uncontrolled (defaultOpen) modes.
